title: Product: Active Pipeline Review
summary: Project updates addressed Lovable reporting and Sophia campaign status. Deep discussion on low customer engagement and product improvements, including security checks for BDC analytics and fine-tuning Sophia for internal deployment before external sales integration.

decisions.description: Lovable report sharing is ready for external integration into the portal using existing dealer ID.
Internal security check for BDC analytics dashboard was positive, internal deployment testing to begin next week.
Sophia campaign priority is internal fine-tuning and deployment for Strawa dealers before external sales.
Proposed testing a shift in Sophia's calling window to 2:00 PM - 8:00 PM to align with customer pickup rates.

actionItems.task: Reschedule meeting with Michael Donovan for tomorrow between 11:00 AM - 11:30 AM.
Provide Vinnie Micciche with Sophia callback data from October through January for tomorrow's call review.
Work to point the callback numbers to Frank's follow-up team to answer.
Investigate Sophia engagement rate decline, including lead quality and phone screeners impact.

expectedOutcome.description: Understand reasons for engagement rate drop and identify improvements.

actionItems.task: Address the 'save a deal' prompt issue to generate comprehensive quantitative reports.

expectedOutcome.description: Accurate and complete quantitative reports from call data.

actionItems.task: Incorporate evaluation tools from the start for the Co-pilot project.

expectedOutcome.description: Monitor product usage and response effectiveness from initial deployment.

tensions: Persistent decline in Sophia engagement rates despite changes and tweaks.
Lack of confidence in current Sophia reporting data accuracy due to significant variations.
Difficulty selling AI tools purely on efficiency versus demonstrating clear ROI.
Staffing challenges for shifting Sophia's calling window to evening hours.
Concerns about AI potentially 'training replacements' versus creating new roles.
Security and cost management concerns with public GPT model usage.
referencesToPast: Michael Donovan requested a reschedule of a meeting from the previous day with Jason Branham.
Sergey Lyapustin confirmed Lovable reports would integrate similar to current Metabase reports using the same dealer ID method.
Kevin Breen confirmed having Sophia performance data from October through January.
Vinnie Micciche mentioned they have talked about Voli replacement software for years.
Vinnie Micciche referenced not liking Sophia's first results in November and then January's data.
Matt Watson explained new AI capabilities for quantitative reports, referencing a prior discussion with Scott.
Vinnie Micciche mentioned showing a report to Sergey Lyapustin this morning.
Matt Watson confirmed they spent six months reviewing dealer prompts to create presets.
Harinder Bhinder mentioned looking at Co-work for analysis capabilities.
Matt Watson mentioned testing Snowflake's AI tool.

contributions.description: Confirmed external report sharing via Lovable using existing dealer ID method.
Reported positive internal security check for BDC analytics dashboard built by Kevin.
Requested Sophia performance data (old and new) for an upcoming call review meeting.
Emphasized Sophia project is not finished, needs tweaking for significant revenue potential.
Asked for clarification on 'done' for Sophia, suggesting Strawa dealer deployment as first step.
Outlined potential revenue projections based on selling Sophia and other services to dealers.
Raised concerns about Sophia engagement rate dropping from 15% to 11%.
Countered that even low engagement can be valuable if it provides marginal benefit over human labor.
Expressed lack of confidence in current reporting data due to significant variations in engagement rates.
Suggested reviewing data query, lead source quality, and phone screeners impact on engagement decline.
Suggested analyzing patterns of who calls back, leading to discussion about Sophia's calling window.
Proposed testing a shift in Sophia's calling window to 2:00 PM - 8:00 PM.
Emphasized incorporating evaluation tools from the start to monitor Co-pilot usage.
Stressed tracing and logging usage for security and managing costs of GPT models.
Suggested using LLMs for data analysis to quickly identify issues humans might overlook.
